title: No rate limiting in starting up a bot
evidence: I was able to start up a bot numerous times. Send to intruder and repeat the step numerous times. I did this 196times
matched_concept: Burp Intruder, repeated bot startup, no rate limiting

title: Resource Consumption DOS on EdgeMax v1.10.6
evidence: this could be fairly bad if made in to a python script along with google dorks and automation. Or even a python script that someone has to only enter in an IP address and it will take the router offline in about 5 minutes
matched_concept: python script, automation, DoS via automation

title: SMS Invite Form Abuse
evidence: whisper.sh fails to protect the invite form from abuse. They could send repeated/automated requests to the same phone number or range of phone numbers by launching automated POST requests
matched_concept: automated POST requests, SMS spam, no rate limiting

title: Exploitable vulnerability in SDEX
evidence: Create 20 accounts and fund with 120 XLM each. Code a primitive bot that submits a transaction with 100 ManageOffer operations for each account every 4 seconds. Rent a cheap cloud server and launch the bot.
matched_concept: trading bot, automated market manipulation, bulk operations

title: Rate limit bypass on contact-us through IP Rotator
evidence: spam the mail box of victim through alternative email and support team via Burp IP Rotator extension
matched_concept: IP rotation, rate limit bypass, contact form spam

title: Abuse of API causing spamming users and possible DOS due to missing rate limit on contact form
evidence: Abuse of Api that causes spamming users and possible DOS due to missing rate limit on contact form

title: Abuse of API causing spamming users and possible DOS due to missing rate limit
evidence: another endpoint doesn't have any rate limit on it to prevent spamming you by posting a lot of questions

title: Bypassing the SMS sending limit for download app link
evidence: an attacker can send the download link sms to any number of people bypassing the sms rate limit imposed by zomato
matched_concept: SMS mass sending, rate limit bypass

title: Reading redacted data via hackbot's answers
evidence: I have found a way to use hackbot's automated duplication answers to reveal redacted data via brute force. By making a new submission with the same subject the hackbot told me that the report is 100% the same
matched_concept: bot brute force, redaction bypass, automated duplication

title: Uber promo endpoint does not implement MFA, blacklisting, or rate limiting
evidence: A massively parallel Golang application was configured to spawn thousands of concurrent HTTP request workers, each with a PRNG-derived token
matched_concept: massively parallel, promo code brute force, no rate limiting

title: Possibility to brute force invite codes in riders.uber.com
evidence: one could brute force invitation codes since there is no protection against brute force attacks
matched_concept: invite code brute force, no rate limiting

title: Bulk UUID enumeration via invite codes
evidence: It is possible to enumerate UUID via invite code. As invite codes are public so attacker can easily enumerate bulk UUID
matched_concept: bulk UUID enumeration, automated enumeration

title: Lack of rate limiting leads to enumeration of promotion codes
evidence: Invite codes are 5 alphanumeric characters. This means there are 36^5 or 60,466,176 possible invite codes
matched_concept: mass enumeration, no rate limiting, promotion codes

title: e.mail.ru: SMS spam with custom content
evidence: unlimited SMS sending to any numbers with custom content control
matched_concept: unlimited SMS spam, custom content

title: Mass SMS flood (wallet.rapida.ru)
evidence: Mass SMS flood via payment template automation

title: No rate limit on forgot password leading to massive email flooding
evidence: Send This Request To Intruder And Repeat It 100+ Times. 100+ Email In Your INBOX. Mass Mailing Or Email Bombing
matched_concept: Burp Intruder, email bombing, no rate limiting

title: Email flooding using user invitation feature in biz.yelp.com
evidence: Mass Email Flooding. Use up system resources for sending emails, possibly DoS or even DDoS
matched_concept: invitation feature abuse, email flooding

title: No rate limiting for password reset email leads to email flooding
evidence: No Rate Limiting implemented in sending the Password Reset Email. attacker can use this Vulnerability to bomb out the Email Inbox of the victim

title: No rate limiting for confirmation email leads to email flooding
evidence: No rate limiting implemented in sending the confirmation email. attacker can use this vulnerability to bomb out the email inbox of the victim

title: Bypass of rate limiting in secure_session endpoint password input
evidence: Rate limiting bypass on password endpoint leading to password disclosure

title: Login page vulnerable to bruteforce attacks via rate limiting bypass
evidence: Login page brute force via rate limiting bypass
matched_concept: brute force, rate limiting bypass

title: Bypass rate limiting on /users/password (site-wide rate limit bypass)
evidence: I noticed when we hit the /users_sign_in endpoint too many times it will give us HTTP/1.1 429 Too Many Requests. Null byte %00 bypass technique.
matched_concept: rate limit bypass, null byte injection, HackerOne itself

title: Bypass GraphQL rate limit by abusing negative cost queries
evidence: each app has a bucket of query cost. Change first parameter into (first: -1000) and pool will be back up to full. It is possible to leverage the logic error to bypass GraphQL rate limiting.
matched_concept: GraphQL rate limit, negative cost, cost bucket manipulation

title: rubygems.org batching attack to confirmation_token by bypass rate limit
evidence: I confirmed that EmailConfirmationsController has the same problem - batching attack to brute force confirmation tokens
matched_concept: batching attack, rate limit bypass, token brute force

title: Rate-limit bypass (2FA bypass on Slack)
evidence: This vulnerability is about a 2FA Bypass. After performing 4-6 failed 2FA Attempt, Rate limit logic will be Triaged. I tested using iOS App and found that API Endpoint /api/auth.signin have no rate limit implemented.
matched_concept: 2FA bypass, rate limit bypass, iOS API endpoint

title: No rate limit leads to OTP brute forcing
evidence: There is no rate limit protection in the endpoint which could lead to brute force otp code

title: Lack of rate limitation allows brute force verification code
evidence: Lack of rate limitation on careers site allows the attacker to brute force the verification code

title: Bypass email verification - no rate limiting for verification code
evidence: It is a 4 digits code. But there is no rate limiting enable while checking the code

title: No rate limiting on phone number login leads to login bypass
evidence: No Rate Limiting On Phone Number Login Leads to Login Bypass - 4-digit OTP brute-forceable

title: Bypass two-factor authentication (Slack)
evidence: will be able to take over slack's 2FA enabled account by brute forcing 2FA code on password reset page
matched_concept: 2FA brute force, password reset page

title: Rate limit function bypass via X-Forwarded-For
evidence: I have added a header X-Forwarded-For: 127.0.0.1 which will bypass the rate limit and reset request limits. Every time rate limit exceeded just change IP to another one.
matched_concept: X-Forwarded-For bypass, rate limit bypass, IP rotation

title: User Enumeration, Information Disclosure and Lack of Rate Limitation on API
evidence: User Enumeration, Information Disclosure and Lack of Rate Limitation on API
matched_concept: API user enumeration, no rate limiting

title: Captcha bypass enables login bruteforce
evidence: There is captcha bypass, which can lead to login credentials bruteforce attack. Just remove g-recaptcha-response from request, and the server accepts your request.
matched_concept: captcha bypass, parameter removal, brute force

title: Registration captcha bypass
evidence: I've found that it is possible to bypass captcha during registration. Attacker can automatize registration process and create multiple accounts.
matched_concept: captcha bypass, automated registration

title: Bypassing captcha in registration on hosted site
evidence: the captcha on the user registration form is very simple and can be easily bypassed to automatically register any number of accounts

title: Captcha bypass at registration
evidence: The captcha is simple enough that can be read by any script

title: Captcha bypass leads to register multiple users with one valid captcha
evidence: captcha now expire and we can use single valid captcha for register and call to many user
matched_concept: captcha reuse, mass registration with single captcha

title: Captcha Bypass in Coinbase SignUp Form
evidence: The g-recaptcha-response is not validated on the server-side when submitting a Signup form. Any or no value can be provided for this header
matched_concept: captcha bypass, server-side validation missing

title: Captcha Bypass With Extension
evidence: there is a Extension Named Rumola Which Automatically Fills up the Captcha While We Fill Other Credentials
matched_concept: browser extension captcha solver, automated captcha bypass

title: IRC-Bot exposes information
evidence: if the bot is online in IRC, you can send him task numbers via private messages, and then he exposes the title of tasks without access control

title: Bot setting information leakage in OpenChat room
evidence: Bot setting information leakage in OpenChat room - no-content visibility but confirmed bot settings leak to unauthorized users

title: SSRF via webhook
evidence: There exists an SSRF vulnerability with the account webhook feature, allowing an attacker to verify the existence of the EC2 metadata url. POC: Create a webhook with url http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/. Trigger this webhook by sending/receiving an email.
matched_concept: webhook SSRF, EC2 metadata, automated trigger

title: SSRF in webhook functionality
evidence: SSRF in webhook functionality on HackerOne itself

title: SSRF vulnerability in gitlab.com webhook
evidence: Login to your GitLab account and create a new project, then go to settings/integrations. You can add url to ssrf.

title: Attacker can delete and read private project webhooks
evidence: An attacker can delete and read webhooks that are configured for all projects on a GitLab instance. This includes private projects. These webhooks contain private API tokens most of the time.
matched_concept: webhook IDOR, private API tokens exposed

title: Order Creation Webhooks can be edited/deleted by staff with Settings-only permission
evidence: A STAFF with just Settings permission can modify Order webhooks if the web-hooks were created previously by Owner or other permitted users
matched_concept: webhook access control, privilege misuse

evidence: API Server DoS (crash?) if many large resources (~1MB each) are concurrently/repeatedly sent to an external Validating WebHook endpoint

title: Remote hacker can download files of master branch using Selenium automation
evidence: I am aware of this bug, so I use a web automation software like Selenium IDE. I can program a script to refresh this page automatically, so if the victim ever clicks the download button, I will be able to download the file
matched_concept: Selenium IDE, browser automation, race condition exploitation

title: API request signature can be reused with other parameters
evidence: It is possible to re-use the signature but with another payload (as the payload is not part of the signature). A read-only API key is created then used to create a trade/withdraw API key.
matched_concept: API signature replay, parameter substitution

title: Apple Pay cryptogram replay and amount tampering
evidence: During Apple Pay in-app or on-site payments the device generates a payment cryptogram. This attack is possible only with Visa cards - cryptogram replayable, amounts tamperable.
matched_concept: payment cryptogram replay, amount tampering

title: xmlrpc.php brute force and denial of service
evidence: This can be automated from multiple hosts and be used to cause a mass DDOS attack on the victim. This method is also used for brute force attacks.
matched_concept: XMLRPC multicall, automated DDoS, brute force amplification
exclusion_reason: Tangential - XMLRPC is a known protocol-level issue, not user-controlled automation in the application logic sense. Pattern is well-documented and repetitive across reports.

title: xmlrpc.php file is enabled - DoS and bruteforce attack
evidence: xmlrpc.php file is enable it will used for (DOS) and bruteforce attack
matched_concept: XMLRPC, DoS, brute force
exclusion_reason: Near-duplicate of 325040 pattern - same XMLRPC multicall vector

title: xmlrpc.php brute force and DoS
evidence: xmlrpc.php could potentially be abused to cause a DDOS attack against a victim host via system.multicall
matched_concept: XMLRPC multicall DDoS

title: xmlrpc.php XSPA, brute force, and DoS
evidence: xmlrpc.php enables attacker to XSPA Brute-force and even Denial of Service(DOS)
matched_concept: XMLRPC, XSPA, brute force, DoS

title: Privilege escalation of external user to internal access through project token
evidence: An external user with maintainer privileges to a project can create a project token which is connected to a Bot with internal access

exclusion_reason: Core bug is privilege escalation via token scoping, not automation abuse

title: H1-415 CTF Writeup - XSS in support team bot
evidence: the support team bot finally resulted in a successful XSS attack via URL-encoded path traversal bypassing CSP on the bot's browser
